prompt:
Perf ticket from the observability side. stitch.ts folds the gateway's audit-log stream (one 'open' entry and one 'close' entry per RPC, keyed by call id) back into call records for the latency dashboard. On staging traffic it was fine; on a production day (~160k entries) the stitch job pegs a core for ages and the dashboard misses its refresh window. The reason is plain once you watch it: every time a close comes in, the resolver walks back through the stored open log entry by entry until it finds the right open — the longer the day, the longer every walk.

Speed it up without moving the semantics one inch. Contract (also documented in the module header): a close consumes the MOST RECENT still-unconsumed open with its id — same-id opens nest like a stack, our retries depend on that; calls come out in close order with { id, method, openTs, closeTs, durationMs, status } and durationMs = closeTs - openTs; unmatchedOpens holds ids of never-consumed opens in open order; orphanCloses holds closes that found nothing, in close order. Output order is load-bearing — downstream diffs snapshots.

Keep the stitch(entries, opts) signature and keep the probe accounting honest: onProbe(candidate.id) must still fire once for every stored open entry you examine while resolving a close. The perf suite budgets total probes at 3x the entry count for the 160k-entry day, so per-close rescans of the log can't pass; examining just the open you actually consume is obviously fine.

node --test test_stitch.ts is the acceptance contract — leave the test file alone.
